# Phase 2 — Runtime Refactor Inventory

**Generated by:** `npm run saas:phase2-runtime-inventory` (live, 2026-05-09).
**Machine output:** `backend/reports/saas/phase2/runtime-inventory.{json,md}`.
**Branch:** `claude/design-multitenant-recruitment-8H42T`.

> Scope: Phase 2 PLANNING only. Nothing here changes runtime behaviour.

---

## 1. Totals

| Metric | Count |
|---|---:|
| Modules scanned | 28 |
| Source files | 201 |
| `prisma.<model>.<op>` direct call sites | **793** |
| Raw-SQL hit sites (`Prisma.raw`/`$queryRaw`/`$executeRaw`) | **17** |
| `@Cron` decorators | 0 |
| `setInterval` at module scope | 1 (notifications) |
| Existing `agencyId` filter usages | ~18 |

## 2. Risk tiers (heuristic from the inventory script + human judgement)

| Tier | Meaning | Count | Move order |
|------|---------|------:|-----------:|
| **P0 — critical tenant-leak risk** | raw SQL OR multi-tenant scheduler OR backup-style global scan | 6 | first |
| **P1 — high risk** | ≥30 Prisma calls AND no agency-scope filter | 5 | second |
| **P2 — medium risk** | 5..29 Prisma calls | 9 | third |
| **P3 — low risk** | <5 Prisma calls | 6 | fourth |
| **global** | utility / common / saas / health | 2 | last; usually no-op |

## 3. P0 modules (raw-SQL or global scan) — refactor first

| Module | Prisma calls | Raw SQL | Why P0 | Required tenant filter |
|---|---:|---:|---|---|
| `reports` | 27 | **10** | The reports engine; `Prisma.raw` is its primary composition surface. **The single largest leakage vector in the codebase.** | `tenants.id` (via primary table's `tenantId` column; agency scope via `agencyId`). See `SAAS_PHASE2_REPORTS_ISOLATION_DESIGN.md`. |
| `applicants` | 50 | **3** | High-volume domain entity, raw SQL for bulk operations | `applicants.tenantId` (added in Phase 1) + `agencyId` for sub-scope |
| `pipeline` | 91 | **1** | Workflow execution touches every domain — broad blast radius | inherits via Employee/Applicant joins; mandatory `tenant_id =` in joins |
| `employees` | 35 | **1** | High-volume domain entity | `employees.tenantId` + `agencyId` |
| `notifications` | 28 | 0 | Has `setInterval(scanAllData, 6h)` — biggest cross-tenant runtime risk | replace `setInterval` with BullMQ per-tenant fanout (ADR-004 §9) |
| `backup` | 0 | 1 | `pg_dump`-style global path; must move under `PlatformAdmin` | platform-admin only; no tenant filter (cross-tenant by design but audited) |

| `settings` | 41 | Branding moves to `Tenant.branding`; system-only settings remain global |
| `workflow` | 35 | Today: `isPublic = true` default. Phase 2 introduces system-template + clone-on-use pattern (ADR-004 §10) |
| `finance` | 32 | Entity-keyed (FinancialRecord); same shape as documents |

## 6. P3 modules

`logs`, `email`, `prisma`, `job-ads`, `app.module.ts`, plus a few utility files (< 5 prisma calls each).

## 7. Global / utility (no tenant impact)

`common/`, `saas/`, `i18n/`. Already untenanted by design.

## 8. Per-module refactor checklist (template)

For each P0 / P1 / P2 module the Phase 2 implementation will:

1. **Inventory** — run `saas:phase2-runtime-inventory` against the module path; record current count baseline.
2. **Replace** direct `prisma.<model>.<op>` with `tenantPrisma.client.<model>.<op>`. The wrapper pass-through means runtime behaviour is identical until `TENANT_PRISMA_ENFORCEMENT=true`.
3. **Audit raw SQL** — every `Prisma.raw` / `$queryRaw*` / `$executeRaw*` is migrated to either:
    - the `SOURCE_DEFS` registry under `backend/src/saas/reports/` (for reports), OR
    - the `tenantPrisma.withTenant(...)` pattern (for one-off interactive tx), OR
    - a typed Prisma method call with no raw escape.
4. **Apply agency scope** — every list/read endpoint targeting an agency-scoped model calls `applyAgencyScope(where, 'agencyId', ctx)` (helper to be added in Phase 2.5).
5. **Audit decorator** — wrap every mutation with `@Audit('module.action')` (interceptor wired in Phase 3 hardening).
6. **Tests** — two-tenant isolation test added under `backend/src/saas/__validation__/<module>.check.ts`; passes BEFORE the wrapper enforces tenant filtering.
7. **Inventory** — re-run; raw-SQL count for the module must drop to 0 unless explicitly allow-listed via `// @tenant-reviewed: <reason>`.

## 9. Recommended phase order (with effort estimate)

| Phase | Modules | Estimated effort |
|-------|---------|------------------|
| **Phase 2.0 — scaffolding** (this PR) | report scaffolding under `backend/src/saas/reports/`; scanners; planning | ½ week |
| **Phase 2.1 — reports refactor** | `reports` | 2 weeks (ADR-007 hard prerequisite to enforcement) |
| **Phase 2.2 — notifications fanout** | `notifications` | 1 week |
| **Phase 2.3 — entity-keyed denorm** ✅ DONE | `documents`, `work_permits`, `visas`, `compliance_alerts`, `financial_records` (+10 descendants) | 15 models migrated additively; 56 rows backfilled, 2 quarantined, 0 mismatches; 4 new READY report sources |
| **Phase 2.4 — domain modules** | `applicants`, `employees`, `pipeline`, `vehicles`, `workflow` | 2 weeks |
| **Phase 2.5 — utility modules** | rest of P2 + P3 | 1 week |
| **Phase 2.6 — RLS audit-mode** | flip `RLS_ENFORCEMENT` to log-only | ½ week + 7 days observation |
| **Phase 2.7 — RLS FORCE** | per-table `FORCE` rollout | ½ week + per-table observation |

Total Phase 2 estimate: **~ 8 weeks** of engineering time, plus
parallel ops (operating system, schema migration, observation
windows).

## 10. Existing `agencyId` usage hot-spots

Modules that already filter by `agencyId` in some paths (≥1 occurrence):

| Module | Direct `where: { agencyId: ... }` usages |
|---|---:|
| `recycle-bin` | 6 |
| `employees` | 8 |
| `notifications` | 3 |
| `reports` | 1 |

These are the **easy wins** for Phase 2.4: the migration is mechanical
because the existing logic already reasoned about agency scope. They
will not need new business logic; they need to switch to the
`tenantPrisma` wrapper and the `applyAgencyScope` helper.

## 11. Modules with NO `agencyId` filter and high `prisma` count

| Module | Prisma calls | Risk |
|---|---:|---|
| `documents` | 44 | entity-keyed; Phase 2.3 |
| `workflow` | 35 | system-template + clone (ADR-004 §10) |
| `vehicles` | 47 | already has `agencyId` on rows; service code may not filter — audit per file |
| `settings` | 41 | mostly global; per-tenant branding moves out |

These are the modules where the **migration risk is highest** — switching to `tenantPrisma` will start filtering rows by `tenantId` (once enforcement turns on) where today there was no filter. Read-equivalence tests (Phase 2 test strategy §5) are the safety net.

## 12. Module-by-module review checklist (for code-owners)

Each P0/P1/P2 module gets a code-owner-led review with these mandatory checks:

- [ ] Every `prisma.X.<op>` replaced with `tenantPrisma.client.X.<op>` OR explicitly allow-listed `// @tenant-reviewed: <reason>`.
- [ ] Every list endpoint that does not require all-tenant data has agency scope applied (where applicable).
- [ ] Every raw SQL site converted or moved into the registry.
- [ ] Two-tenant isolation test exists and passes pre-enforcement.
- [ ] Read-equivalence test passes against pre-Phase-2 baseline (same query, same row count, same row IDs for the active tenant).
- [ ] Raw-SQL scanner reports `0 BLOCKER` (unreviewed) findings for the module after the refactor.
